title: Bagheswari Temple, Bongaigaon
tagline: Bagheswari Temple, Bongaigaon — spiritual town of Assam

overview.short: The Bagheswari Temple is a Hindu temple dedicated to the mother goddess Bagheswari, one of the oldest of the 51 Shakti Pitha.
overview.description: The Bagheswari Temple is a Hindu temple dedicated to the mother goddess Bagheswari, one of the oldest of the 51 Shakti Pitha. Situated in southern part of Bongaigaon city in Assam, India. It is an important pilgrimage destination for general Hindu.

topPlaces.name: Akana (village)
topPlaces.description: Akana (also known as Akna) is a village of Nalbari district in Western Assam under 14 No Pub Banbhag Gram Panchayat.

topPlaces.name: Udiana
topPlaces.description: Udiana is a village in Kamrup rural district, situated in north bank of river Brahmaputra, surrounded by Goreswar and Baihata.

topPlaces.name: Harisinga
topPlaces.description: Harisinga is a town in the Udalguri district in the northeastern Indian state of Assam. It is in the Bhergaon subdivision of Udalguri district.

topPlaces.name: Bangaon (Kamrup)
topPlaces.description: Bangaon is a Block(MCB) in Kamrup rural district, in the state of Assam, India, situated in north bank of river Brahmaputra.

topPlaces.name: Patkata, Assam
topPlaces.description: Patkata is a village in Ghograpar Subdivision, Nalbari District, Assam, India. It is located near the district boundary with Kamrup District, about 8 kilometres east of the district seat Nalbari, and 1 kilometres west of the subdivision seat Ghograpar.

topPlaces.name: Gopalpur, Kamrup
topPlaces.description: Gopalpur is a village in Kamrup rural district, in the state of Assam, India, situated in north bank of river Brahmaputra.

topPlaces.name: Tarani
topPlaces.description: Tarani is a village in Kamrup rural district, situated in north bank of river Brahmaputra, surrounded by Goreswar and Baihata.

topPlaces.name: Bardal (Kamrup)
topPlaces.description: Bardal is a village in Kamrup rural district, in the state of Assam, India, situated in north bank of river Brahmaputra.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Bagheswari Temple, Bongaigaon?
faq.a: The best time to visit Bagheswari Temple, Bongaigaon is Oct – Mar. Summers are 25–42°C and winters are 7–24°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Bagheswari Temple, Bongaigaon?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Lokpriya Gopinath Bordoloi International Airport, Guwahati (40 km) and the nearest railway station is Guwahati Railway Station (37 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Bagheswari Temple, Bongaigaon?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Bagheswari Temple, Bongaigaon at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Bagheswari Temple, Bongaigaon?
faq.a: Stays in Bagheswari Temple, Bongaigaon start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Bagheswari Temple, Bongaigaon?
faq.a: The top attractions are Akana (village), Udiana, Harisinga, Bangaon (Kamrup), Patkata, Assam.
